title: DRAFT: [3/2] [Moore] Lower class object access and virtual dispatch through LLVM memory
body:
Introduce an explicit LLVM-backed object-memory path for Moore classes and
carry virtual dispatch cleanly through `convert-moore-to-core` and
`convert-to-llvm`.

This change keeps class handles as `!moore.class<...>` values and class
variables as `!moore.ref<class<...>>`, while using `!moore.ptr<T>` for
addresses into object storage. `moore.class.property_ref` now models field
address computation explicitly, and Moore-to-core lowers class field reads
and writes through LLVM pointer semantics instead of LLHD probing.

On the lowering side, class objects use the stabilized header ABI with
RTTI and vtable metadata, symbolic vtables are materialized as LLVM
globals, and virtual calls lower to an explicit vtable slot lookup plus
indirect `llvm.call`. Class-handle variables no longer introduce extra
heap allocation in `VariableOpConversion`; uninitialized handles become
null LLVM pointers and reads of handle values are treated as identity on
the LLVM path.

The importer and verifier logic are updated to understand the split
between variable refs and object pointers, and the test suite is refreshed
to use the current explicit assembly syntax for `moore.read` and the
assignment ops.

Add and expand coverage for:
- import of class field accesses using `moore.ptr`
- nontrivial virtual method bodies with real arithmetic/control-flow
- end-to-end virtual dispatch through `circt-verilog --ir-moore`,
  `--moore-create-vtables`, `--convert-moore-to-core`, and `--convert-to-llvm`
- parser/roundtrip tests for the updated Moore assignment assembly forms
